string.name: encrypt_option_use_aescrypt_desc
string.value: AESCrypt is an open source file encryption format based on AES encryption. It is also portable, can be decrypted with AESCrypt programs on other platforms.

string.name: encrypt_option_use_azecrypt_desc
string.value: Amaze's Encryption Format is also based on AES encryption; but different from AESCrypt format, it can only be decrypted by this device; also you must not uninstall Amaze File Manager from this device nor change the way you lock screen, otherwise you will have no way to recover the encrypted file.

string.name: ftp_prompt_accept_first_start_saf_access
string.value:
Since this is your first run of the FTP server, and you are using the device's internal storage as shared folder, please allow Amaze to access the storage using SAF.\n\n

You only need to do this once, until the next time you select a new location for sharing.

string.name: ftp_prompt_accept_first_start_saf_access_title
string.value: Grant SAF access for FTP server

string.name: ftp_prompt_restart_server
string.value: You will need to restart the FTP server for changes to take effect.

string.name: ftp_server_fallback_path_reset_prompt
string.value: FTP server shared path had been reset to internal storage as you are switching back to legacy filesystem implementation. Please select a new path using the menu on the top right as necessary.

string.name: ftp_server_prompt_connect_to_network
string.value: Connect to a network or enable AP

string.name: ftp_server_reset_notify
string.value: FTP server shared path had been reset to internal storage

string.name: ftp_server_root_filesystem_warning
string.value: You are choosing a path which requires root access, which enables read/write operations in the whole filesystem on your device, and it is dangerous - careless mistakes could brick your device!\n\nAre you sure?

string.name: ftp_server_root_unavailable
string.value: You are choosing a path which requires root access. Please enable root explorer in settings if your device is rooted.

string.name: grant_all_files_permission
string.value:
Since Android 11, Google requests File Managers to request user permission for managing all files on the device. Details here.
        

Amaze File Manager needs this permission too. After pressing "Grant"here, please select Allow access to manage all files option on the next screen.
        

Canceling this dialog will exit the app.

string.name: ssh_connect_failed_host_key_changed_message
string.value:
IT IS POSSIBLE THAT SOMEONE IS DOING SOMETHING NASTY!

Someone could be eavesdropping on you right now (man-in-the-middle attack)! It is also possible that the host key has just been changed. The fingerprint for the public key sent by the remote host does not match the one stored in Amaze.

Please contact your system administrator or setup a new connection to continue connecting to the server.

string.name: ssh_connect_failed_host_key_changed_prompt
string.value:
IT IS POSSIBLE THAT SOMEONE IS DOING SOMETHING NASTY!

Someone could be eavesdropping on you right now (man-in-the-middle attack)! It is also possible that the host key has just been changed. The fingerprint for the public key sent by the remote host does not match the one stored in Amaze.

You may proceed with updating host fingerprint or cancel updating SSH connection settings.

string.name: ssh_connect_failed_host_key_changed_title
string.value: WARNING: REMOTE HOST IDENTIFICATION HAS CHANGED!

string.name: ssh_host_key_verification_prompt
string.value:
The authenticity of host '%1$s' can't be established.

%2$s key fingerprint is %3$s.

Please tap "Yes" to confirm identity; otherwise, please tap "No".
